prerequisites: A Navan account that can create API credentials in the Navan platform.
A Navan API credential pair (client_id and client_secret) generated from the Navan platform. By default new credentials are read-only.
A start_date value marking the earliest date from which the connector should replicate data.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 client credentials
authMethods.summary: Navan uses the OAuth 2.0 client-credentials flow. Provide the client_id and client_secret created in the Navan platform. The connector exchanges them at the Navan token endpoint (POST https://app.navan.com/ta-auth/oauth/token for the US region, or https://app-fra.navan.com/ta-auth/oauth/token for the EU region) with grant_type=client_credentials to obtain a bearer access token, which is then sent as Authorization: Bearer <token> on every API request. Tokens are valid for 12 hours by default; a new token is requested after expiry.

setupSteps.title: Create API credentials in Navan
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Navan platform and create your API credentials following Navan's API Credential Creation Guide. This produces a client_id and client_secret. By default the credentials are granted read-only access; select any additional scope (for example Expense: Write) at creation time only if your use case requires it.

setupSteps.title: Provide the client_id and client_secret
setupSteps.body: Enter the client_id and client_secret from your Navan credentials into the connector. These are exchanged automatically against the Navan OAuth token endpoint (grant_type=client_credentials) to mint a short-lived bearer access token. Do not send an explicit scope parameter when requesting the token, since that overrides the scopes configured on the credential.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date field to the earliest date from which the connector should begin replicating Navan data. Records dated before this value are not synced.

sources.title: Navan Expense API authentication (OAuth 2.0 client credentials, token endpoints, scopes)
